name: Jimmy Yancey
description: African American boogie-woogie pianist, composer and lyricist (1898-1951)
abstract: James Edward Yancey (February 20, c. 1895 – September 17, 1951) was an American boogie-woogie pianist, composer, and lyricist.

concerts.label: Kid Ory and His Creole Jazz Band

works.t: Make Me a Pallet on the Floor

about_quotes.speaker: John "Knocky" Parker
about_quotes.evidence: Closer to Yancey, yes, than before. ... Yancey and, uh, oh, uh, who was that other one? ... We had, uh, the, uh, Railroad Express, that old fellow who created Walking Base before Yancey.

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: I never saw him anyplace. I don't think I ever saw Jim. I saw him maybe...I was out at the ball park with Mayo, but I never heard him play.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: Solo Art was -- at that time he had recorded four black pianists, Cripple Clarence Lofton, Jimmy Yancey, Pete Johnson, Albert Ammons...

about_quotes.speaker: Sweet Williams
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, take Jimmy Yancey or... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, but yeah, but yeah, but now that them guys and Lucky Lewis, them guys.

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: I took some myself. For instance, the Dodds picture and the Jimmy Yancey, maybe one or two others.
